opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I'm covered head-to-toe in these huge, itchy welts that keep moving around my body. I feel like my skin is burning and I'm terrified my throat is going to close.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A rapidly spreading, intensely itchy welts all over my body — I'm scared it's a dangerous allergic reaction.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden itchy welts on the abdomen 6 hours ago, spreading
symptom_narrative.character: large itchy raised welts that come and go, moving around the body
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: individual welts fade within hours while new ones appear elsewhere
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: welts with a pale centre and red flare; no lip/tongue/throat swelling, no breathlessness, no dizziness or vomiting

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a new brand of ibuprofen taken ~12 hours ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: took a new NSAID before the rash started

communication_style.typical_message_style: panicked, fearing a dangerous allergic reaction

objective_data_offered.value: widespread itchy raised welts with a pale centre and red flare that move around and fade within hours; no scaling or blisters
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: camera view; consistent with a histamine welt rash

objective_data_offered.value: speaking in full sentences, no lip/tongue/throat swelling, no wheeze, no dizziness/vomiting
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-report; such a welt rash can evolve, so airway must be safety-netted
